# feat(dataflow): Phase 7.2 + 7.6 — correlation ID ContextVar + centralized URL masking

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `59f34d89a9ef` — feat(dataflow): Phase 7.2 + 7.6 — correlation ID ContextVar + centralized URL masking

**Body**:

Phase 7.2 — correlation ID infrastructure:

Adds ``dataflow/observability/correlation.py`` with a ContextVar-
based binding for per-request correlation IDs. Every log line
emitted inside a scoped block can now read ``get_correlation_id()``
and attach it as an ``extra={"correlation_id": ...}`` field so
multi-step requests no longer interleave in the log aggregator.

API surface (``dataflow.observability``):
- ``get_correlation_id()`` — returns the bound ID or None
- ``set_correlation_id(value)`` — binds for the current task
- ``clear_correlation_id()`` — explicit teardown
- ``with_correlation_id(value)`` — context manager that binds +
  restores on exit (preferred entry point; reset-on-exception safe)

The ContextVar is per-asyncio-task so concurrent requests handled
by the same worker never cross-contaminate their correlation IDs.

Phase 7.6 — centralized URL masking:

Promotes ``_mask_url`` from ``fabric/cache.py`` to
``dataflow/utils/masking.py`` so every module imports the canonical
implementation. ``mask_url`` + new ``mask_secret`` helper. Keeps a
backwards-compatible re-export alias in ``fabric/cache.py`` so the
three existing fabric import sites continue to work.

Total: 490 passing (was 480 — Phase 7.2 adds 10 correlation tests).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
